subject: Sociology
title: Surveys and Sampling
description: A quick win: understand Surveys and Sampling.

hook.text: A 1936 poll predicted Landon would crush Roosevelt. He lost in a landslide. The sample was rich people with phones.

buildup.text: Surveys are sociology's workhorse. But a survey is only as good as the sample it draws from.

discovery.text: Random sampling gives every person an equal chance of selection, making results generalizable to the whole population.

twist.text: Even good surveys have bias. How you word a question can shift responses by 20 percentage points or more.

climax.text: The Literary Digest poll of 1936 surveyed two million people and got it wrong. Gallup polled 50,000 and nailed it.

punchline.text: Sample quality beats sample size. Every single time.

quiz.question: Why is random sampling important in surveys?
options: It makes results generalizable to the whole population
It reduces the cost of research
